equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI and its coverage assertions are absent from the audited repos.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing deterministic 500,000,000,000 wei DA error Phase 2 fee constant and assertions that it is covered by all relevant Phase 1 fees.
related_but_insufficient_code: The audited port constants define FEE_PER_POLL_BLOCK_WEI and settlement offsets but no DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI.
No compile-time or unit assertions compare a 500,000,000,000 wei error fee against image/audio/video/FHE Phase 1 escrow floors in audited repos.
The only DA error constant hits are in a non-audited legacy checkout, not in the requested repo set.

rationale: The merge keeps DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI and compile-time assertions that bound it by image/audio/video Phase 1 settlement fees, with comments stating it lets DA error deliveries settle instead of being skipped. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Multimodal and FHE Phase 2 delivery results that carry hasError=true for DA/storage failures must use the deterministic DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI value of 500,000,000,000 wei, and that constant must not exceed the minimum Phase 1 settlement fees for image, audio, video, or FHE-backed delivery.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create multimodal and FHE Phase 2 delivery results with hasError=true due to DA upload failure and assert builder and verifier settle them using 500,000,000,000 wei, and add compile-time or unit checks that the constant remains <= every relevant Phase 1 escrow floor.
summary: DA error covered-constant fee support is absent from the audited repos.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Add a unit check for DA_ERROR_PHASE2_FEE_WEI <= image/audio/video/FHE Phase 1 fees; it cannot be written against current audited code because the constant is absent.
title: DA error deliveries have covered constant fee
